creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Adam Weinberger from comment #7)
> Which port(s) are you referring to?
Particularly, `net/storj' and `misc/fq' of mine which require populating GH_TUPLE.

> I mean, the "correct" way these days to handle vendored dependencies is not
> to use the vendored dependency system in the first place.  While go
> now recommends that basically nobody use the old vendored dependency system
> at all anymore, we're pretty well stuck regarding old ports that don't use it.
I'm not sure I quite understand.  I've always assumed that Go programs' distfiles can come in two forms: with all dependencies baked into them (aka "vendored" distfile) or they'd be fetched from GitHub/etc. during the build, which we of course cannot and do not want to do, hence we collect that information before the build and populate GH_TUPLE so all dependencies are put in place during `make extract'.

> Depending on how many there are, the right solution may wind up being that we
> simply don't support ports that use the old system.  We have some workarounds
> to allow ports using the "new" module system to build in poudriere, but I'm
> not confident that it'll ever really be possible to "fix" the old system for
> FreeBSD.
Admittedly I don't know much about these old and new systems, I've simply noticed that existing GH_TUPLE is not enough with Go 1.23+ and other ports' maintainers already started to put pre-generated `modules.txt' in the ${FILESDIR} which I could've also done if it weren't that ugly.

$ find . -name modules.txt
./biology/ncbi-entrez-direct/files/modules.txt
./devel/task/files/modules.txt
./multimedia/navidrome/files/modules.txt
./security/picocrypt/files/modules.txt
